info.title: SchemaCheck API
info.description: Validate Schema.org structured data in one API call. Send a URL or raw JSON-LD, get back errors, warnings, rich result eligibility, and fix suggestions. Based on Schema.org vocabulary V29.4 (827 types, 1,528 properties) and Google's current rich result requirements.

externalDocs.description: Full documentation — quickstart, error reference, code examples, and supported schema types

ApiKeyHeader.name: x-api-key
ApiKeyHeader.description: Pass your API key in the x-api-key header.

ApiKeyQuery.name: access_key
ApiKeyQuery.description: Pass your API key as the access_key query parameter (GET requests only).

severity.description: 'error' means a required property is missing or malformed — this will prevent the schema from qualifying for rich results. 'warning' means a recommended property is missing — rich results may still appear, but with reduced quality or features.

property.description: The Schema.org property name that has an issue (e.g. 'author', 'datePublished', 'offers'). Use this field for programmatic checks — it is stable across API versions.

message.description: Human-readable description of the problem. Do not switch on this field programmatically — use 'property' instead, as message wording may change.

fix.description: Actionable instruction for resolving the issue. Written to be understandable by a developer or passed directly to an LLM for automated remediation.

google_docs_url.description: Direct link to the relevant section of Google's structured data documentation for this schema type.

type.description: The Schema.org @type found in this JSON-LD block (e.g. 'Article', 'Product'). Subtypes like NewsArticle or BlogPosting are normalized to their parent type for validation purposes.

valid.description: true if all required properties defined by Google's structured data guidelines are present and correctly typed. A valid schema may still have warnings for missing recommended properties.

rich_result_eligible.description: true if this schema meets the minimum requirements for Google to display an enhanced rich result in Search. A schema can be valid but not rich-result-eligible (e.g. Product without offers or image).

deprecated.description: true if Google has retired or restricted this schema type. Deprecated types are still validated, but with a deprecation_note explaining the restriction.

deprecation_note.description: Explanation of why this type is deprecated or restricted. For example, FAQPage is restricted to government and health websites since 2024; HowTo was retired in August 2024.

errors.description: Required properties that are missing or malformed. Each error prevents the schema from qualifying for rich results. Fix all errors before deploying.

warnings.description: Recommended properties that are missing. Warnings don't block rich results, but including these properties improves the quality and appearance of rich result features.

properties_found.description: Top-level Schema.org property names found in this schema block.

properties_missing_required.description: Required property names that are absent. Use this for programmatic checks — e.g. check if 'offers' is in this array for a Product schema.

properties_missing_recommended.description: Recommended property names that are absent. Adding these improves schema quality and rich result appearance.

ValidationSummary.description: Aggregate statistics across all schemas found on the page or in the request.

total_schemas.description: Total number of JSON-LD blocks found and validated.

valid_schemas.description: Number of schemas with no required-property errors.

invalid_schemas.description: Number of schemas with at least one required-property error.

total_errors.description: Sum of all errors across all schemas on the page.

total_warnings.description: Sum of all warnings across all schemas on the page.

rich_result_eligible.description: Number of schemas that meet Google's rich result eligibility requirements.

score.description: Overall schema health score from 0 to 100. 100 means all required and recommended properties are present. Use as a threshold in CI checks — e.g. fail the build if score < 80.

ValidationMeta.description: Metadata about the validation request itself — timing, caching, and credit usage.

api_version.description: The API version that processed this request.

validated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the validation was performed.

cached.description: true if this result was served from the 1-hour URL cache. Cached results return instantly and cost 0 credits. Use result.meta.cached in monitoring dashboards to distinguish fresh from cached data.

credits_used.description: Number of credits consumed by this request. 0 for cached results, errors (4xx/5xx), and rate-limited requests. 1 for successful validations.

credits_remaining.description: Credits remaining in the current billing period for this API key.

response_time_ms.description: Total server-side processing time in milliseconds, excluding network latency.

url.description: The URL that was validated (present for URL requests).

code.description: Machine-readable error code. Stable across API versions.

post.description: Sign up with an email address and receive a free API key (100 validations/month). No password required.
Passwordless login — returns the API key and usage stats for the given email address.
Creates a Stripe checkout session for plan upgrades. Requires a valid API key. Returns a Stripe-hosted checkout URL.

url.description: Stripe-hosted checkout URL. Redirect the user here.

get.description: Returns all schema types that SchemaCheck can validate, grouped by status (supported, coming_soon, planned, deprecated). No authentication required. Call this before validate_schema to check if a given type is supported. Useful for AI agents to discover capabilities before making validation calls.

200.description: List of schema types with metadata

types.description: All schema types SchemaCheck knows about, across all tiers and statuses.

also_matches.description: Subtypes that are validated using this type's rule file (e.g. Article also validates NewsArticle, BlogPosting).

status.description: 'supported' = validates now. 'coming_soon' = in active development. 'planned' = on the roadmap. 'deprecated' = validated with a deprecation warning.

tier.description: Development tier: 1 = supported, 2 = coming soon, 3 = planned, 4 = future, 0 = deprecated.

google_docs_url.description: Link to Google's structured data documentation for this type.

required_properties.description: Property names that must be present for the schema to be considered valid. Only populated for Tier 1 (supported) types.

recommended_properties.description: Property names that Google recommends but doesn't strictly require. Missing these triggers warnings, not errors. Only populated for Tier 1 (supported) types.

note.description: Optional note about restrictions, deprecation status, or upcoming changes.

total_coming_soon.description: Number of types in active development (Tier 2).

total_planned.description: Number of types on the roadmap (Tier 3 + 4).

total_deprecated.description: Number of types that are deprecated but still validated with warnings.

schema_org_version.description: The Schema.org vocabulary version this API is based on.

last_updated.description: Date the type list was last updated.

get.description: Fetch the HTML at the given URL, extract all JSON-LD blocks, and validate each schema. Results are cached for 1 hour — cached responses cost 0 credits.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The fully-qualified HTTP or HTTPS URL to validate.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: API key (alternative to x-api-key header). Recommended for GET requests.

422.description: URL fetch failed or no JSON-LD found

post.summary: Validate a URL or raw JSON-LD
post.description: Pass a URL (same as GET) or a raw JSON-LD object/array for direct validation. Raw JSON-LD is never cached.

jsonld.description: Raw JSON-LD object or @graph array. Never cached.

422.description: URL fetch failed, no JSON-LD found, or parse error
